description:
Offered: every semester
This course is a survey of global musical traditions with an emphasis on the social, political, and cultural contexts that make each unique. Students will explore how music, as a human phenomenon, and how it may be used to express different ethnic, spiritual, economic, and political viewpoints. Students will develop the vocabulary necessary for critically discussing the fundamental building blocks of music.
Note: Prior musical ability or knowledge is not required for this course.
Meets General Education Requirement(s): DP, FA
